search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG249 falls risk screening prediction tools comprehensive assessment 2025
site:doh.gov.ae falls prevention older adults UAE guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic falls-risk score or risk category
exercise, home-hazard, medicine-change, vitamin, vision, or referral advice generated without assessment
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: NG249 explicitly advises against prediction tools for predicting falls.
Interventions must follow individual assessment and clinician decision.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: No legacy item in gp-falls-risk-screening remains source-supported after conservative correction; item-specific population applicability was not independently established without new research.
setting_applicability: No legacy item in gp-falls-risk-screening remains source-supported after conservative correction; transfer from the recorded source setting to the exact workflow item was not independently established.
UAE_applicability: insufficient_evidence for every removed gp-falls-risk-screening legacy-item mapping; UAE applicability requires independent item-level review before any mapping is reinstated.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact case-identification section supports documenting prior-fall details and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use; it does not classify an individual as high risk.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact comprehensive-assessment section supports clinician-entered cardiovascular, cognition, mood, diet, fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait, balance, hearing, conditions, medicines, neurology, osteoporosis, continence, vision, examination, and concern only where appropriate and assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, prior falls, injury, loss of consciousness, gait, balance, frailty, medicines, vision, home hazards, cognition, assessment results, intervention, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
